evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my Capacitor app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyApp.js, and shows the setup checklist (packages to install, npx cap sync, camera permission entries).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core, scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode, and npx cap sync)
Setup checklist mentions iOS Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription
Setup checklist mentions Android camera permission is handled automatically by the plugin
await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present
ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is awaited before any other Scandit call (semantic)
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
A license key placeholder string is present (semantic)
new BarcodeCapture(settings) is present (v8 constructor used, not BarcodeCapture.forContext)
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present
context.setMode( is called to register the BarcodeCapture mode with the context
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
DataCaptureView.forContext( is present
view.connectToElement( is called to mount the view to a DOM element
new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present
view.addOverlay( is called
Camera.withSettings or Camera.default is present
setFrameSource(camera) is called on the DataCaptureContext (the receiver may be named context or dataCaptureContext) (semantic)
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called to start the camera
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called to register the scan listener
Listener object has a didScan callback

Imports come from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core (DataCaptureContext, ScanditCaptureCorePlugin, Camera, FrameSourceState, DataCaptureView) and scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode (BarcodeCapture, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, BarcodeCaptureSettings, Symbology)

evals.prompt: Add BarcodeCapture barcode scanning to ProductListApp.js. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedProducts array and re-render the product list.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeCapture to ProductListApp.js alongside the existing DOM rendering code. All existing DOM hooks, state, and render functions are preserved.

assertions.text: await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new BarcodeCapture(settings) is present
context.setMode( is called
DataCaptureView.forContext( is present
view.connectToElement( is called
new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present
view.addOverlay( is called
barcodeCapture.addListener( is present
didScan callback is present
didScan callback pushes / appends to scannedProducts
Existing scannedProducts array is preserved
Existing renderProductList function is preserved and called after each scan
Existing updateTotal / total-label DOM update logic is preserved
Existing clearButton click handler is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Capacitor app, I want to do a slow database lookup before re-enabling scanning. Disable the BarcodeCapture mode while handling the scan, then re-enable it once the lookup is done. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false at the start of the didScan callback, performs the lookup, and sets isEnabled = true after the lookup completes.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside didScan before the lookup
A database lookup or async work step is present (e.g. await fetch, await lookup, setTimeout, or similar)
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to add a rectangular viewfinder to the BarcodeCapture overlay so users know where to aim. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a RectangularViewfinder to overlay.viewfinder and imports it from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: overlay.viewfinder = new RectangularViewfinder( is set (or assigned via an intermediate variable)
RectangularViewfinder is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to filter scans so duplicates within 500 milliseconds are ignored in my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before constructing the BarcodeCapture mode.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 500 is set
codeDuplicateFilter is set before new BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to restrict scanning to a small circular area in the center of the camera view in my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app. Use a radius location selection.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RadiusLocationSelection and assigns it to settings.locationSelection before constructing the BarcodeCapture mode.

assertions.text: settings.locationSelection = new RadiusLocationSelection( is set (or assigned via an intermediate variable)
RadiusLocationSelection, NumberWithUnit, and MeasureUnit are imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
locationSelection is set before new BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Customize the BarcodeCapture feedback in my Capacitor app — disable the default sound and keep only the vibration. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill modifies barcodeCapture.feedback so that success has only vibration and no sound.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback is assigned, either via direct assignment or via mutating barcodeCapture.feedback.success (semantic)
new Feedback( is constructed with Vibration.defaultVibration and null for sound (semantic)
Feedback and Vibration are imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when my Capacitor scanning view is destroyed — turn the camera off, disable BarcodeCapture, and detach the DataCaptureView from the DOM.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off), barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false, and view.detachFromElement() into a teardown function.

assertions.text: camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the teardown path
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set in the teardown path
view.detachFromElement() is called in the teardown path
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to enable composite codes (Type A and Type B) in my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app, alongside the symbologies they require. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill calls enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes for the chosen composite types and sets enabledCompositeTypes on the BarcodeCaptureSettings.

assertions.text: settings.enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes( is called with [CompositeType.A, CompositeType.B]
settings.enabledCompositeTypes is assigned [CompositeType.A, CompositeType.B]
CompositeType is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode
Both calls happen before new BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Use ScanIntention.Manual for my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app instead of the default Smart algorithm. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.scanIntention to ScanIntention.Manual before constructing the BarcodeCapture mode.

assertions.text: settings.scanIntention = ScanIntention.Manual is set
ScanIntention is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
scanIntention is set before new BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to show an aimer viewfinder on my Capacitor BarcodeCapture overlay so users have a clear aiming point. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a new AimerViewfinder to overlay.viewfinder and imports AimerViewfinder from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: overlay.viewfinder = new AimerViewfinder( is set (the receiver may be window.overlay or overlay) (semantic)
new AimerViewfinder() is constructed with no arguments
AimerViewfinder is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a laser line viewfinder to my Capacitor BarcodeCapture overlay. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a new LaserlineViewfinder to overlay.viewfinder and imports LaserlineViewfinder from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: overlay.viewfinder = new LaserlineViewfinder( is set (the receiver may be window.overlay or overlay) (semantic)
new LaserlineViewfinder() is constructed with no arguments
LaserlineViewfinder is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app I only want to accept barcodes whose data starts with 'ABC'. Other codes should be ignored and not highlighted. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill inspects barcode.data inside didScan, and for non-matching codes sets the overlay brush to a transparent brush and returns early without processing the code.

assertions.text: barcode.data?.startsWith('ABC') is checked inside didScan (the optional chaining and exact prefix string may vary) (semantic)
For a rejected barcode the overlay brush is set to Brush.transparent (semantic)
For a rejected barcode the callback returns early before processing the code (semantic)
Brush is imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Change the highlight color of recognized barcodes in my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app to a semi-transparent green fill with a green stroke. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a new Brush built with Color.fromHex colors to overlay.brush and imports Brush and Color from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: overlay.brush = new Brush( is set (the receiver may be window.overlay or overlay) (semantic)
Color.fromHex( is used to build the fill or stroke color
Brush and Color are imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable the full ASCII extension for Code 39 in my Capacitor BarcodeCapture app. Use IntegratedApp.js.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code39 symbology settings via settingsForSymbology and calls setExtensionEnabled('full_ascii', true) before constructing the mode.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.Code39) is called
setExtensionEnabled('full_ascii', true) is called on the Code39 symbology settings
The extension is enabled before new BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, view, overlay, listener) is preserved
